expectations.text: Correctly classifies the energy company as an Essential Entity (EE) under NIS2 Annex I (energy sector, electricity sub-sector)
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly classifies the company as Essential Entity (EE) under Annex I, citing electricity distribution system operators as directly named in Annex I, including a classification table confirming the result.

expectations.text: Confirms the entity meets size thresholds (≥50 employees OR ≥€10M turnover) making it automatically in scope
expectations.evidence: Response confirms 200 employees and €50M turnover both exceed the medium-entity thresholds (≥50 employees OR ≥€10M turnover) by a wide margin, with a clear table.

expectations.text: Explains Art. 20 governance obligation: management body approval, training, and potential personal liability
expectations.evidence: Response covers Art. 20 obligations including management body approval of cybersecurity measures, mandatory cybersecurity training, and personal liability under German transposition law (NIS2UmsuCG).

expectations.text: Lists all 10 Art. 21 cybersecurity risk management measures or explicitly covers the key ones (incident handling, BCP, supply chain, MFA, cryptography)
expectations.evidence: Response covers all 10 Art. 21 measures with energy/OT-sector-specific emphasis on SCADA environments, supply chain (grid equipment vendors), and emergency communications.

expectations.text: States Art. 23 incident reporting timelines: 24-hour early warning, 72-hour notification, 1-month final report to CSIRT/competent authority
expectations.evidence: Response clearly states all three Art. 23 reporting stages: 24h early warning, 72h incident notification, and 1-month final report to BSI/CERT-Bund.
